description:
A solar project developer needs a complete techno-economic feasibility package for a proposed 100 MW DC single-axis tracking photovoltaic plant near Phoenix, Arizona. Using PySAM, conduct a three-phase analysis:

Phase 1 - System Design Optimization: Sweep DC-to-AC ratios of 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, and 1.5 to identify the ratio maximizing annual specific yield (kWh/kWp). Using the optimal ratio, compute P50 and P90 annual energy yields by applying root-sum-square of four uncertainty components: inter-annual weather variability (4.5%), model uncertainty (3.2%), module degradation uncertainty (2.0%), and plant availability uncertainty (1.5%).

Phase 2 - Financial Structuring: Configure a PySAM Single Owner financial model with the optimal system design: $950/kW installed cost, $12/kW-yr fixed O&M, 30% ITC, 5-year MACRS, 21% federal + 8.84% state tax, 60% debt at 5.5% interest, 18-year term, 0.5%/yr degradation, 25-year analysis. Solve for the PPA price ($/kWh) achieving 10.0% after-tax equity IRR. Report the project NPV, real LCOE (cents/kWh), and minimum annual DSCR.

Phase 3 - Investment Sensitivity: Run 9 scenarios: installed costs {$800, $950, $1100}/kW against PPA prices {$0.030, $0.035, $0.040}/kWh. For each, report after-tax IRR (%) and 25-year NPV ($). Determine the break-even installed cost at PPA=$0.035/kWh where NPV is approximately zero.

Save all results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/phoenix_feasibility_package.json with three top-level keys: system_design, financial_analysis, and sensitivity_analysis.
